title: Revert to v44 — Remove all PDF integration and canister audit changes

requirements.summary: Remove the 'Attach PDF' icon button from all Quotations Fund transaction rows in FundsManagement.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Attach PDF' button or file input appears on any transaction row in FundsManagement.tsx
No hidden file input elements with accept='.pdf' remain in the component

file_operations.description: Remove all PDF attachment UI elements from transaction rows: delete the 'Attach PDF' icon button (Paperclip icon), remove the hidden file input element with accept='.pdf', and remove all event handlers related to PDF file selection. The transaction rows must return to their pre-PDF state showing only transaction data (date, type, amount, description, category) without any PDF-related controls.

requirements.summary: Delete the usePdfAttachments custom React hook file
acceptanceCriteria: The file `frontend/src/hooks/usePdfAttachments.ts` no longer exists in the project
No imports of `usePdfAttachments` remain anywhere in the codebase

file_operations.description: Delete the entire usePdfAttachments hook file that provides IndexedDB-based PDF storage functionality (savePdf, getPdf, deletePdf, hasAttachment) keyed by transaction ID. This hook was added during the PDF integration phase and must be completely removed.

requirements.summary: Remove all usePdfAttachments hook wiring and PDF UI logic from FundsManagement.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: Transaction rows in FundsManagement.tsx show no PDF-related buttons (Attach, View, Download)
No paperclip icon or green checkmark attachment indicator appears on any transaction row
No calls to savePdf, getPdf, deletePdf, or hasAttachment exist in FundsManagement.tsx
The component compiles and renders without errors

file_operations.description: Remove all usePdfAttachments hook integration: delete the import statement for usePdfAttachments, remove the hook initialization and all destructured functions (savePdf, getPdf, deletePdf, hasAttachment), delete the handleFileSelect function that calls savePdf, remove all PDF-related UI elements from transaction rows including the 'View' button (eye icon) that opens PDFs in new tabs, the 'Download' button (download icon) that triggers file downloads, and any conditional rendering based on hasAttachment (paperclip or green checkmark indicators). Restore transaction rows to their v44 baseline state showing only the standard transaction data columns without any PDF attachment features.

requirements.summary: Revert canister ID audit changes to vite.config.ts, .env, and .env.production back to v44 state
acceptanceCriteria: vite.config.ts canister ID injection logic matches the v44 state
Any .env or .env.production files modified during the canister audit are restored to their v44 state
No canister audit-specific overrides or patches remain outside of the immutable useActor.ts file

file_operations.description: Revert the canister ID resolution logic to the v44 baseline state. This includes restoring the original canister_ids.json and .dfx/local loading logic, removing any audit-specific fallback mechanisms, canister ID validation code, or explicit canister ID overrides that were added during the v45+ canister audit work. Ensure the configuration matches exactly how it loaded canister IDs in the last stable v44 version before the PDF integration began.

Revert any canister-related environment variable changes made during the canister audit work (v45+) back to the v44 baseline. Remove any explicit CANISTER_ID_* overrides, audit flags, or debugging variables that were added after v44. If the file did not exist in v44 or contained no canister audit-specific content, restore it to that state or delete audit-specific entries.

Revert any production canister ID configuration changes made during the canister audit work back to the v44 baseline state. Remove any explicit canister ID overrides, fallback logic references, or audit-specific production environment variables that were introduced after v44. Restore the file to match its exact v44 state before the PDF integration and canister audit phases.
